methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axibo AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ishaan Mehta that has raised C$12M to date. The valuation universe for comparison includes 9 peers across the robotics and manufacturing sector: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms, with notable private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this case. The analysis identified 11 same-sector peers in robotics and manufacturing, but filtering by stage (seed), adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier yielded fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for a robust median calculation. Relying on an unfiltered sector median would introduce excessive dispersion and reduce analytical precision given the stage and capital raise heterogeneity within the broader robotics sector.

The lack of sufficient narrow-set comparables limits the strength of direct multiple-based valuation. A credible valuation range for Axibo AI would require either (1) expansion of the comp set through primary research on earlier-stage robotics investments, (2) application of venture-specific methods such as venture capital method calculations based on target exit scenarios, or (3) forward-looking revenue multiples if operational metrics are available. Key uncertainties center on market timing, technical differentiation within a crowded robotics landscape, and capital efficiency relative to the C$12M raised.
